description_long: This is an irregularly shaped, pale stone with a distinctive honeycomb-like surface texture, featuring numerous rounded depressions and cavities across its main face. The stone appears to be naturally weathered, with a rough, pitted exterior and some areas showing a chalky, whitish coloration. One side of the stone is more solid and less perforated, with a blocky protrusion that looks as if it was once attached to a larger mass. The overall form is elongated and slightly curved, with the perforated surface dominating one side and the more massive, broken section on the other. The coloration ranges from off-white to light gray, with subtle hints of greenish or brownish weathering in some of the cavities. This stone would likely be found as a decorative or functional barrier in an urban park or garden, where its unique texture and form could serve both aesthetic and practical purposes.
description: An elongated, pale stone with a honeycomb-like, pitted surface and a rough, weathered appearance. One side is more solid and blocky, contrasting with the perforated main face.
description_view_0: Shows the elongated, pitted surface with deep cavities and a prominent blocky protrusion on the right.
description_view_1: Highlights the more solid, less perforated side with a rough, broken texture and fewer visible cavities.
description_view_2: Displays the full length of the stone's honeycomb surface, emphasizing the depth and regularity of the pits.
description_view_3: Focuses on the broad, pitted face, showing the uniformity and density of the rounded depressions.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or used as a low barrier along a path
attribute_pattern: Distinct honeycomb pattern of rounded pits and cavities, irregular but visually striking, covering most of the main surface.
attribute_weathering: Rough and pitted with signs of natural erosion, some areas appear chalky and slightly discolored, indicating prolonged exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with other stones or landscaping elements, may be partially surrounded by soil or low vegetation, providing a subtle contrast to manicured urban features.
